content:
# Kiosk Opening and Closing Checklist

**Tool ID:** T204-02
**Block:** B204 (Kiosk Operations)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** All Staff
**Time Required:** 15–20 minutes (opening); 15–20 minutes (closing)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

A kiosk has no margin for error. There is no back-of-house to retreat to, no second barista to cover while you sort out a forgotten task. If the milk is not in the fridge when you open, you lose your first 20 customers while you figure it out. This checklist ensures the kiosk is fully loaded at opening and properly secured at closing — every single day.

No shortcuts. No "I'll do it later." The list is the list.

**Built for All Staff working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A comprehensive opening and closing checklist specifically designed for kiosk and compact-format cafe operations. Covers all tasks required to go from locked-up to service-ready (opening) and from last customer to fully secured (closing). Accounts for kiosk-specific constraints: limited space, self-contained utilities, and solo operation.

This tool supports **Kiosk Operations** (B204) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** All Staff (whoever opens or closes the kiosk)
**Time Required:** 15–20 minutes opening; 15–20 minutes closing
**Frequency:** Every trading day. Non-negotiable.

**What You'll Need:**
- This checklist (laminated and permanently fixed inside the kiosk)
- Whiteboard marker (wipe and reuse daily)
- Keys, alarm code, and access to utilities

**Steps:**
1. Arrive at scheduled time (30 min before opening; stay 20 min after last customer at close)
2. Work through every item in sequence
3. Tick each item as completed
4. Note any issues
5. Sign off

---

## Checklist

### OPENING CHECKLIST

#### Date: ________ | Opener: ________ | Opening Time: ________

#### 1. Access & Safety (2 minutes)

- [ ] Unlock kiosk / open shutters / deactivate alarm
- [ ] Visual safety check: no damage, no leaks, no unusual conditions
- [ ] Check weather conditions — set up outdoor elements if appropriate
- [ ] Verify fire extinguisher present and accessible

#### 2. Utilities (3 minutes)

- [ ] Power on — confirm all circuits working (no tripped breakers)
- [ ] IF self-contained water: check fresh water tank level — minimum 50% for opening (*_____ L*)
- [ ] IF self-contained water: check waste water tank — must have capacity for full day (*_____ %* full)
- [ ] IF mains water: verify flow — run tap for 5 seconds
- [ ] Heating/cooling on if needed (fans, heater, AC)
- [ ] Lighting on — customer-facing and work area

#### 3. Espresso Machine & Grinder (10 minutes)

- [ ] Espresso machine on — allow warm-up time (15–20 min)
- [ ] Flush each group head (10 seconds)
- [ ] Check boiler pressure gauge: 1.0–1.2 bar
- [ ] Load beans into main grinder hopper
- [ ] Dial in: pull 2–3 test shots (18g in / 36g out / 25–28 sec)
- [ ] Taste test — flavour acceptable? Adjust grind if needed
- [ ] Record grind setting: _____
- [ ] Load decaf grinder, dial in if applicable
- [ ] Steam wand: purge, check pressure, wipe

#### 4. Stock Check (5 minutes)

- [ ] Milk stocked in fridge: whole ___L, oat ___L, soy ___L, almond ___L
- [ ] Fridge temperature: _____ C (must be 1–5 C)
- [ ] Takeaway cups: 8oz ___ pcs, 12oz ___ pcs (enough for full day?)
- [ ] Lids stocked (flat + dome)
- [ ] Syrups at station, levels checked
- [ ] Hot chocolate / matcha powder stocked
- [ ] Sugar, sweetener, stirrers available
- [ ] Napkins stocked
- [ ] Paper bags available
- [ ] Pastries / food set out in display (if applicable)
- [ ] Count opening pastry stock: _____ pcs (for end-of-day waste tracking)

#### 5. Service Setup (3 minutes)

- [ ] POS system on, float counted: EUR _____
- [ ] Card terminal on, charged, connected
- [ ] Delivery tablet on (if applicable)
- [ ] Cups, lids, sleeves positioned within arm's reach
- [ ] Menu board / price list displayed and correct
- [ ] Customer-facing counter clean and clear
- [ ] Waste bin lined and positioned
- [ ] Sanitiser and clean cloth at station
- [ ] A-board / signage placed outside (if applicable)

#### 6. Final Check

- [ ] All items above completed
- [ ] Any shortages noted and communicated: ________________
- [ ] Ready to serve first customer

---

### CLOSING CHECKLIST

#### Date: ________ | Closer: ________ | Last Customer: ________

#### 1. Last Customer & Sales Close (3 minutes)

- [ ] Last customer served
- [ ] POS closed — end-of-day report pulled
- [ ] Revenue recorded: EUR _____
- [ ] Transaction count: _____
- [ ] Cash counted and reconciled: EUR _____ (variance: _____)
- [ ] Cash secured / deposited per protocol

#### 2. Waste & Stock (3 minutes)

- [ ] Unsold pastries/food counted and logged in waste log: _____ pcs = EUR _____
- [ ] Any expired stock disposed and logged
- [ ] Remaining milk noted: whole ___L, oat ___L, soy ___L
- [ ] Remaining cups noted: 8oz ___, 12oz ___
- [ ] Restocking list written for tomorrow's opening
- [ ] Perishables stored correctly in fridge (labelled, dated)

#### 3. Espresso Machine & Grinder Cleaning (5 minutes)

- [ ] Backflush each group head with cleaning detergent (5 cycles per group)
- [ ] Remove and rinse portafilter baskets
- [ ] Steam wand: soak tip, wipe, purge
- [ ] Drip tray emptied and cleaned
- [ ] Grinder hoppers emptied — beans stored in sealed container (dated)
- [ ] Grinder chute brushed out
- [ ] Machine exterior wiped down
- [ ] IF machine turned off overnight: switch off (check venue protocol)

#### 4. Cleaning (5 minutes)

- [ ] All surfaces wiped with sanitiser
- [ ] Milk jugs washed and dried
- [ ] Cups, tools, and utensils washed and stored
- [ ] Blender disassembled and washed (if applicable)
- [ ] Counter customer-facing side cleaned
- [ ] Floor swept / mopped
- [ ] Waste bin emptied, new bag fitted
- [ ] Recycling separated (cups, cardboard, general)
- [ ] Cloths replaced (dirty cloths in wash bag)

#### 5. Utilities & Security (3 minutes)

- [ ] IF self-contained water: note remaining fresh water level: _____ L
- [ ] IF self-contained water: note waste water level: _____ % (empty if > 80%)
- [ ] Fridge set to overnight mode (or confirmed running)
- [ ] All non-essential power off (lights, POS, card terminal, signage)
- [ ] Heating/cooling off
- [ ] A-board / signage brought in and stored
- [ ] Shutters closed / kiosk locked
- [ ] Alarm activated

#### 6. Handover Note

| Item | Notes for Next Opener |
|------|----------------------|
| Stock shortages | *e.g., Only 2L oat milk remaining — order needed* |
| Equipment issues | *e.g., Grinder running slightly fast today — monitor* |
| Customer feedback | *e.g., Regular asked about cold brew — consider for summer?* |
| Other | |

#### Sign-Off

| Shift | Name | Time | Signature |
|-------|------|------|-----------|
| Opening | | | |
| Closing | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Water Tank Runs Dry Mid-Service

**Why it happens:** Solo operator too busy serving to check the water level. Tank capacity underestimated for the day's volume.
**How to prevent:** Check water level at opening and at the mid-point of your shift. Know your tank's capacity in litres and how many drinks that translates to (roughly 1 litre of water per 4–5 espresso drinks).
**If it's already happening:** Set a phone alarm for mid-shift water check. Arrange a refill protocol (who delivers water, how long it takes).

### Risk 2: Cash Not Reconciled at Close

**Why it happens:** Tired after a solo shift, just want to lock up and go. Cash count feels like extra work.
**How to prevent:** Cash reconciliation is closing task #1 — do it immediately after the last customer, while the POS is still open and fresh in your mind. Takes 3 minutes.
**If it's already happening:** If cash is consistently short, check for counting errors, pricing errors on POS, or unlogged refunds.

### Risk 3: Cleaning Skipped Because "Nobody Sees the Inside"

**Why it happens:** The kiosk looks fine from the customer side. The inside accumulates coffee grounds, spilt milk, and sticky syrup.
**How to prevent:** Hygiene is non-negotiable — for food safety, equipment longevity, and your own working comfort. A dirty kiosk also smells, and customers notice. VMVT inspectors certainly will.
**If it's already happening:** Schedule a deep clean this week. Then maintain with the daily closing checklist — it prevents the buildup from ever happening.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Indoor Mall Kiosk:** Add mall-specific requirements: shutter protocols, shared waste collection, mall management check-in.
**Outdoor Container:** Add weather-related tasks: wipe condensation, check drainage, secure loose items in wind.
**Market Stall:** Combine with setup/teardown checklists (see T205-01 for mobile operations). Prioritise portable equipment checks.
**Window Hatch:** Add external counter cleaning and hatch mechanism check to both opening and closing.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate this checklist and fix it permanently inside the kiosk
- Use a whiteboard marker so it can be wiped and reused daily
- Add your specific equipment names and quirks
- Include emergency contact numbers for equipment failures, water delivery, and management

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T204-01 — Kiosk Workflow Layout Planner (L1, template)
- T204-03 — Daily Kiosk Stock Planner (L2, tracker)
- T204-04 — Kiosk Operations SOP (L2, SOP)
- T204-05 — Kiosk Performance Dashboard (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B089

**Parent block:** B204 — Kiosk Operations
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
